task:
Atom D (ticket 0025, bug): When createWorkspace itself git-initializes a non-git primary root, baseline-commit the user's FULL existing working tree (honoring .gitignore) — not just the cocoder/** zone. Do NOT re-baseline an already-git repo.

OWNER: packages/daemon/src/routes.ts:752-776 (createWorkspace). The `initializedRepo` flag is already computed at line 756 (`!(await ctx.git.isGitRepo(primaryRoot))`). The seeded root .gitignore is added at lines 759-762. The current governance commit at line 769 only commits `scaffolded.map(...)` paths.

WHAT TO CHANGE:
- Only when `initializedRepo === true`: after the scaffold + .gitignore seeding (so .gitignore is in place and honored), stage the whole tree with `git add -A` and make a baseline commit (e.g. message `chore: import existing tree (baseline)`) BEFORE/AROUND the existing cocoder governance commit. The .gitignore is the ONLY exclusion mechanism — rely on it, do not assume tree cleanliness. The cocoder governance commit (line 769) must still happen and still be reported in the receipt.
- When the root was ALREADY a git repo (`initializedRepo === false`): behavior is UNCHANGED — no baseline import, only the cocoder/** governance commit.
- Add the baseline-commit primitive to the git interface used via `ctx.git` if one does not already exist; reuse the existing git wrapper module (look at where isGitRepo/initRepo/commitGovernance live) and match its style. NEVER add a remote and NEVER push — local commit only.

MUST NOT BREAK:
- The `auditWriteBoundary` / cocoder-only trust boundary is about what CoCoder MODIFIES, not what git TRACKS — this baseline import only snapshots files the user already authored; CoCoder still edits only cocoder/**.
- governanceCommitted / governanceCommittedSha receipt fields and the existing create-workspace success/error flow stay intact.
- Already-git onboarding path: zero change.

ACCEPTANCE (provide evidence — run it yourself):
- A daemon real-git test: create a workspace on a non-git fixture that contains product files PLUS a node_modules/ dir; assert (a) the product files are tracked after create, (b) node_modules/ is NOT tracked (excluded by seeded .gitignore), (c) the cocoder/** zone is committed. Add it alongside the existing daemon real-git tests that cover governanceCommitted/branch main.
- An existing-git fixture test still shows NO baseline re-import (only the cocoder governance commit) — extend/verify the existing coverage.
- `git status` on the non-git fixture is clean of the user's content after create.
- Run the daemon test suite + `tsc --noEmit` and report exit codes.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/** (routes.ts, the git wrapper module, and the daemon real-git tests). Do not touch other packages. This is a one-shot atom (judgment-gated verify).

Atom E (ticket 0026, bug): Complete the scaffold governance commit list. The scaffold writes cocoder/workspace.json and cocoder/counters.json into the target's cocoder/ zone, but they are NOT in the committed file-list, so a fresh workspace create leaves them untracked (confirmed on Job Hunt commit 2ef1de1). This applies to BOTH the new-primary (git-init) and onboard-existing (already-git) paths, since both commit only the `scaffolded` list returned by scaffoldWorkspaceGovernance.

OWNER: scaffoldCocoderZone in packages/core/src/scaffold/scaffold.ts (the source of the `created` list) and its consumer scaffoldWorkspaceGovernance at packages/daemon/src/routes.ts:362-369 (returns `created.map(...)`, which feeds the governance commit at routes.ts:769). The files are written via packages/core/src/store/portable/paths.ts (workspaceFile=cocoder/workspace.json, countersFile=cocoder/counters.json).

WHAT TO CHANGE — make the commit list COMPLETE and the disposition EXPLICIT:
- Investigate every cocoder/** file the scaffold actually writes (trace scaffoldCocoderZone + any workspace.json / counters.json writers it triggers). For EACH such file, ensure it is EITHER (a) included in the returned `created` set so it enters the governance commit, OR (b) explicitly added to cocoder/.gitignore as genuine runtime churn. No scaffold-written cocoder/** path may be left silently untracked.
- At minimum cocoder/workspace.json and cocoder/counters.json must end up tracked (committed) — they are governance identity/state (workspace.json = {schemaVersion,id,name}); CoCoder's own repo tracks counters.json. Prefer committing over ignoring unless a file is pure per-run churn (decide per file; document the call in the diff).
- Do NOT regress the existing 16 committed files, the .gitignore seeding, or the Atom D baseline-commit behavior (already committed at ad457ebc). Note: on the non-git path the Atom D `git add .` baseline would now also catch these, but the governance commit itself must still be complete — fix the `created`/commit list, do not rely on the baseline as a backstop, because the already-git path has no baseline commit.

MUST NOT BREAK: ordinary (non-scaffold) commit flows; the governanceCommitted/Sha receipt; runner portable-history scope (runner.ts already lists cocoder/workspace.json + counters.json as PORTABLE_RUN_HISTORY_SCOPE — stay consistent, don't double-own).

ACCEPTANCE (provide evidence — run it yourself):
- A test asserting that after a fresh workspace create, the governance commit's tracked set equals the scaffold's WRITTEN set minus the cocoder/.gitignore-IGNORED set — i.e. no scaffold-written cocoder/** file is left untracked. Add/extend it in the daemon real-git create-workspace tests (alongside the Atom D coverage in packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts), exercising the already-git path so the assertion does not depend on the baseline commit.
- Explicitly assert cocoder/workspace.json and cocoder/counters.json are committed (tracked in HEAD) after create.
- Run the daemon test suite + core test suite + `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck`; report exit codes.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/scaffold/** , packages/core/src/store/portable/** (only if a writer must be adjusted), packages/daemon/src/routes.ts (scaffoldWorkspaceGovernance only), and the daemon/core tests. One-shot atom (judgment-gated verify).

Atom F (ticket 0027, task): Generalize the onboard-existing priority TEMPLATE so a non-code repo (content / ops / docs, or a mix) is a FIRST-CLASS onboarding target, not an override the orchestrator must rescue every time. The live Oscar already adapted for Job Hunt (a markdown-driven operating system); bake that into the template text.

OWNER (primary): templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md.

WHAT TO CHANGE in that template:
- Objective (line 8): generalize the opening from 'An existing repo (real product code, no cocoder/ governance yet)' to cover an existing repo that is code, content, ops/docs, OR a mix. Likewise relax 'world-class, multi-pass code review + audit' so 'code review' is not the only framing (e.g. 'review + audit' that covers code AND content/governance subsystems). Keep the 'world-class / never one cheap pass / big lift' bar.
- Decomposition: make SUBSYSTEM TYPING explicit. In step 2 (Recon) and step 3 (Dual-source deep read), say the audit must detect and type subsystems as code subsystems vs content/governance/ops subsystems and scope the reads accordingly (the live run_178 recon already proposed exactly this split — markdown-OS + thin JS layer). Treat content/governance subsystems as distinct, intended read targets.
- Evidence rule: generalize every 'file:line' occurrence (Verified-when line 10; step 4 line 22; 'Quality is mechanism' line 29) from 'file:line evidence' to 'path (and line where it applies)' so markdown trackers/docs are first-class evidence, not second-class. Keep the rule's force: a finding with no path evidence is unverified.
- PRESERVE exactly: the cocoder/** trust boundary (frontmatter auditWriteBoundary + the hard trust-boundary paragraph), the founder gates (recon/spend -> questions -> ratify), dual-source fan-out + adversarial cross-check, and the 'never touches product code / proposes not seizes' stance. No regression for genuine CODE repos — the code-subsystem path must remain fully specified, not watered down.

ALSO: investigate whether a synced copy or a test pins this template. (a) If cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md (this repo's live copy) is a verbatim seed of the template, apply the SAME generalization so they do not drift; if it is a distinct dogfood instance with its own content, leave it and say so. (b) grep the test suites for any test that parses/pins onboard-existing.md (e.g. like the 'base ad-hoc priority template parses and stays product-generic' test in packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts) and run it; if a test asserts product-generic phrasing or frontmatter, keep it green (the generalization should HELP product-genericness, not break it).

ACCEPTANCE (provide evidence):
- Show the template Objective + decomposition no longer presuppose product code, and quote the new language that tells the audit to TYPE subsystems (code vs content/ops) and scope reads accordingly.
- Confirm frontmatter (id, title, auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"]) is intact and the file still parses as a valid priority (run loadPriority-backed tests / the template-parse test).
- Run any test touching the template + the daemon suite section that scaffolds/parses templates; report exit codes. No regression for code repos.

WRITE SCOPE: templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md (primary); cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md ONLY if it is a verbatim template seed that must stay in sync; and any test file that pins the template. Do not touch packages/** source logic. One-shot atom (judgment-gated verify).

Atom F, RE-SCOPED (ticket 0027). The previous attempt was REJECTED: the template content edits were correct, but the repo keeps the onboarding priority as TWO byte-identical copies and you edited only one, then weakened the guard test. Fix that. (The prior attempt's edits may still be in your working tree — keep the good template prose, but you MUST also do the two corrections below.)

BACKGROUND — two synced copies (verify this yourself):
- templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md  <- what scaffoldCocoderZone seeds into onboarded repos.
- packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md               <- the shipped base copy (basePrioritiesDir()).
The repo deliberately keeps these BYTE-IDENTICAL: adhoc-session.md is identical across both today, and onboard-existing.md was identical at HEAD before the prior attempt. The original scaffold test asserted `seeded === readFile(basePrioritiesDir/onboard-existing.md)` — that was a real CROSS-COPY SYNC GUARD, not a tautology.

GOAL (same content generalization as before — content/ops/docs repos as first-class; subsystem typing code vs content/governance/ops; evidence rule file:line -> 'path (and line where it applies)'; PRESERVE the cocoder/** trust boundary, founder gates, dual-source + cross-check, big-lift framing, and the code-repo path), applied CORRECTLY:

1. APPLY THE IDENTICAL EDIT TO BOTH COPIES. After your edits, `diff templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md` MUST report NO differences (byte-identical), exactly as adhoc-session.md is.
2. RESTORE A REAL SYNC GUARD in packages/core/tests/scaffold.test.ts. Do NOT compare the seeded file against the template it was seeded from (that is a tautology). Either keep the original assertion (seeded === basePrioritiesDir copy) OR add an explicit assertion that the two on-disk copies are byte-identical. The test must FAIL if the two copies ever drift again. You may keep the added content assertions (the 'code, content, operations/docs, or a mix' and 'type subsystems...' contains-checks) — those are fine — but the drift guard must be genuine. Restore the `basePrioritiesDir`-based helper if you removed it.

ACCEPTANCE (provide evidence — run it yourself):
- `diff` of the two onboard-existing.md copies prints nothing (identical).
- scaffold.test.ts contains an assertion that genuinely fails on cross-copy drift (show the assertion), and is green now.
- Both copies still parse as a valid priority with frontmatter id/title + auditWriteBoundary: ["cocoder/**"] intact (loadPriority-backed).
- Run packages/core tests + packages/daemon tests + `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck`; report exit codes. No regression for code repos.

WRITE SCOPE: templates/workspace-cocoder/cocoder/priorities/onboard-existing.md, packages/personas/base/priorities/onboard-existing.md, packages/core/tests/scaffold.test.ts. One-shot atom (judgment-gated verify).

Atom G (ticket 0028, bug): Founder-facing run labels must use the PER-ROOT display number, not the global run_${seq} id. A fresh root's first run should read as 'Run 1' / '#1' everywhere the founder sees it, while the global run_${seq} stays the internal unique key (storage, joins, filenames). NOTE: the ticket's exact line numbers have drifted since D-F committed — locate the sites by symbol/string, not line number.

REFERENCE (already correct, reuse its derivation — do NOT add a second copy): packages/ui/src/renderer/adapter.ts uses `runDisplayNumber(r)` and renders `displayName = displayNumber === null ? r.id : 'Run ' + displayNumber`. The per-root number is `portableRunDisplayNumber` / run.json `displayNumber` (allocated in packages/core/src/store/portable/run-creation.ts; available in runner.ts as `portableRunDisplayNumber`).

FORMAT DECISION (apply consistently):
- Human-readable founder surfaces (run record header, oz-chat replies, oz-host / context-pointer slug labels): show `Run {displayNumber}` when the per-root number is known, falling back to the global `run.id` only when it is null (mirror adapter.ts exactly).
- Commit-message trailers (`via CoCoder run ${run.id}` and the run-history `run-history: ${run.id} via CoCoder run ${run.id}` in runner.ts): lead with the per-root number BUT keep the global id present for uniqueness/traceability — format `run {displayNumber} ({run.id})` (e.g. `... via CoCoder run 1 (run_178)`). Do NOT drop the global id from trailers.

LEAK SITES TO FIX (find current locations by string):
- packages/core/src/runner/record.ts — run record header `# Run ${run.id}`.
- packages/core/src/runner/runner.ts — the commit-message trailer strings `via CoCoder run ${run.id}` (atom commits + run-history commit). `portableRunDisplayNumber` is in scope there.
- packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts — replies like `${run.id} is ${run.status} ...`.
- packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts and packages/daemon/src/oz-context-pointer.ts — slug labels.

ONE-OWNER REQUIREMENT (elegance): there must be a SINGLE derivation of the founder-facing label / displayNumber, reused by all surfaces above. If `runDisplayNumber` (or an equivalent) lives only in the UI adapter and core/daemon cannot import it, factor the derivation into a shared owner (e.g. packages/core) and have adapter.ts, record.ts, runner.ts, and the daemon surfaces all consume that one owner. Do NOT copy the 'Run N / fallback to id' logic into each file. For record.ts and the daemon surfaces, source displayNumber from the canonical accessor (store/portable run.json), not by re-parsing run-dir names ad hoc.

MUST NOT BREAK:
- The internal unique id is unchanged: storage keys, joins, portable run-dir names (`{displayNumber}-{run.id}`), and filenames keep using run.id. No cross-workspace collisions.
- If ANY code PARSES a commit trailer to recover run.id (grep for `via CoCoder run` consumers / commit-link extraction), the new `run {displayNumber} ({run.id})` form must still let it recover run.id — update the parser if needed and keep it green.
- Runs with a null/absent displayNumber (e.g. legacy/non-portable) still render via the run.id fallback — no crashes.

ACCEPTANCE (provide evidence — run it yourself):
- A test pinning that founder-facing labels derive from `displayNumber` (not `run.id`): assert the run record header, an oz-chat reply, and a commit-message trailer all show the per-root number for a run whose displayNumber=1 (and that the trailer still contains the global run_id in parens).
- A fallback test: displayNumber null/absent → surfaces fall back to run.id without error.
- Run packages/core + packages/daemon test suites + `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck`; report exit codes.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/core/src/runner/** , packages/core/src/store/portable/** (only if the shared accessor must live there), packages/daemon/src/oz-chat.ts, packages/daemon/src/oz-host.ts, packages/daemon/src/oz-context-pointer.ts, packages/ui/src/renderer/adapter.ts (only if factoring the shared owner), and the corresponding test files. One-shot atom (judgment-gated verify).

Atom G, RE-SCOPED (ticket 0028). Your prior attempt was excellent and is MOSTLY right — KEEP all of it (the shared display.ts / run-display.ts owner, labels.ts, record.ts, runner.ts trailers, oz-chat/oz-host/oz-context-pointer/oz-awareness, adapter.ts de-dup, and the new tests). It was rejected for ONE narrow same-class miss; fix exactly that, do not redo the rest.

THE MISS: packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts (~line 848) still emits a founder-facing commit trailer with the BARE global id:
  message: `oscar-post-wrap: ${run.priorityId} via CoCoder run ${runId}`
This post-wrap support commit is a commit the founder reads in git history — same defect class as the runner.ts trailers you already fixed. It must use the per-root reference like the others.

FIX:
1. In launcher.ts, derive the per-root reference for this run and use it in that trailer: resolve displayNumber via the daemon helper you already have (withPortableDisplayNumber(ctx, run)), then build the trailer with coCoderRunReference(...) so it reads `oscar-post-wrap: ${run.priorityId} via CoCoder run {displayNumber} ({runId})` (and falls back to the bare runId when displayNumber is null). `ctx` and `run`/`runId` are in scope at that call site.
2. Update the test that pins the OLD bare format: packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts:1415 currently expects `oscar-post-wrap: demo via CoCoder run ${run.id}`. Update it to assert the new per-root form WITH the global id preserved in parens (e.g. matches `oscar-post-wrap: demo via CoCoder run \d+ \(${run.id}\)` when displayNumber is set), consistent with how runner.test.ts pins the runner trailers. Keep/добавь a null-displayNumber fallback assertion if that test path can hit it.
3. FINAL SWEEP: grep the daemon + core src for any OTHER founder-facing `via CoCoder run ${...}` (or run-label) site still using the bare global id, and fix any you find the same way. (Test FIXTURE strings that merely pass a message INTO runCommitGate — e.g. commit-gate.test.ts:235 — are NOT production trailers; leave those.) Confirm in your report that launcher.ts was the only remaining production leak, or list any others you fixed.

MUST NOT BREAK: everything from the prior attempt stays green; internal run.id unchanged for storage/paths/joins; trailers still contain the global run_id in parens so any parser can recover it.

ACCEPTANCE (provide evidence — run it yourself):
- Show the launcher.ts trailer now uses coCoderRunReference and the updated mutations.test.ts assertion.
- State the result of the final sweep (launcher.ts only, or the full list fixed).
- Run packages/core + packages/daemon test suites + `pnpm --filter @cocoder/core --filter @cocoder/daemon typecheck`; report exit codes.

WRITE SCOPE: packages/daemon/src/launcher.ts, packages/daemon/tests/mutations.test.ts, plus any additional production leak the sweep finds (daemon/core src) and its pinning test — and you retain the files from the prior attempt. One-shot atom (judgment-gated verify).
